mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions force wage growth that expands consumer purchasing power, directly boosting corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts surge while residential credit contracts signals speculative leverage unwinding, which historically precedes broad equity drawdowns and reduces forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward 60-day returns are not significantly lower when HOUST rises and RSAFS falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 115 during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and input cost inflation that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO breaks above 115, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth acceleration directly increases near-term corporate revenue expectations, causing immediate equity price appreciation and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no correlation with GDP growth surprises, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of positive growth acceleration instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.3 percent month-over-month forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite sustained inflation spikes above the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth coinciding with stable unemployment confirms sustainable labor demand, which supports consumer-driven earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series move favorably, or forward 60-day returns are not significantly higher during confirmed labor demand expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid housing recovery during post-shock phases signals broad economic normalization and rising consumer confidence, lifting cyclical equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative when housing starts accelerate sharply, or the signal shows no predictive power in a rolling 2-year window.
